generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor receiving input current (I_M) and producing motor torque (τ_M) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance GearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gear transmission component converting motor torque (τ_M) to gearwheel torque (τ_G) with rotational energy flow."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox converting gearwheel torque (τ_G) to system output torque (τ_out) with rotational energy flow."
]

instance SiemensSinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller executing motion commands, processing feedback, and applying control algorithms for the first axis."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorShaftEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on motor shaft providing angular position feedback with ±0.1mm positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorShaftEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind gearbox providing secondary angular position feedback with ±0.06mm path repeatability."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor current sensor measuring input current (I_M) streamed via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorShaftEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorShaftEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams describing structural composition of motor, gear transmission, and cycloidal gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance SimulinkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) implemented in MathWorks Simulink for real-time friction characterization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulinkDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-provided dynamics models for torque/velocity derivation from encoder data in Simulink."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance FrictionlessBaselineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Frictionless baseline model (r_M, r_G, r_out) for comparative analysis with friction-inclusive models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance FrictionInclusiveModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Friction-inclusive model (τ̃_out) incorporating Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, or LuGre friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MotorSimulationModule : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation module (Simulation_M) computing motor torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) from input current (I_M)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance GearwheelSimulationModule : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation module (Simulation_GW) computing gearwheel torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) from motor output."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance GearboxSimulationModule : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation module (Simulation_GB) computing system output torque (τ_out) and angular velocity (ω_out) from gearwheel input."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of measured motor current (I_M) from MotorCurrentSensor."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor torque (τ_M) computed from motor current (I_M) using manufacturer dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftEncoderData, MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedMotorAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor angular velocity (ω_M) computed from motor shaft encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedGearwheelTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived gearwheel torque (τ_G) computed from motor torque (τ_M) and gear transmission ratios."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftEncoderData, SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedGearwheelAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived gearwheel angular velocity (ω_G) computed from secondary encoder data and transmission ratios."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedSystemOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived system output torque (τ_out) computed from gearwheel torque (τ_G) and cycloidal gearbox ratios."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DerivedSystemOutputAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived system output angular velocity (ω_out) computed from secondary encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionlessMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated frictionless motor torque (r̃_M) from frictionless baseline model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionlessGearwheelTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated frictionless gearwheel torque (r̃_G) from frictionless baseline model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorShaftEncoderData, SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionlessOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated frictionless system output torque (r̃_out) from frictionless baseline model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedFrictionCompensatedOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction-compensated system output torque (τ̃_out) from friction-inclusive model (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, etc.)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool for structural/behavioral modeling using Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCharacterizationService, SystemDecompositionService
]

instance SimulinkTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink for executable friction/dynamics simulations and real-time model execution."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCharacterizationService, ModelComparisonService, ParameterOptimizationService
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, encoder angles) to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTraceabilityService
]

instance ModelBasedSystemsEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) with RFLP methodology for system decomposition into requirements, functions, logics, and physics."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemDecompositionService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) for structuring data traces, sources, and processing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTraceabilityService, ModelComparisonService
]

instance PurposeDrivenDigitalShadowMethodology : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Purpose-driven digital shadow creation methodology (4-step: problem identification, asset/model analysis, model creation, data identification)."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCharacterizationService, ParameterOptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionCharacterizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time multi-physics-aware detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in gearbox using white-box and black-box models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionComponentIdentificationInsight, FrictionModelParameterUpdateAction
]

instance DataTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Full provenance tracking of data points via Digital Shadow Reference Model, linking sources to processed outputs."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSourceProvenanceInsight
]

instance ModelComparisonService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of Simulink-based friction models to compute deviations between measured and simulated torque/angular velocity."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueDeviationAnalysisInsight, AngularVelocityDeviationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard displaying time-series data, model outputs, and deviation metrics (e.g., error between τ_out and τ̃_out)."
    DTDFVocab:provides UnifiedDataVisualizationInsight
]

instance ParameterOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identification of friction model parameters (Stribeck curve coefficients) for dynamic adaptation to wear, load, or temperature changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizedFrictionParametersInsight, AdaptiveControlRecommendationAction
]

instance SystemDecompositionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "System decomposition using MBSE and RFLP methodology for structuring the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemComponentStructureInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionComponentIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components via model-subtraction techniques (τ_out - τ̃_out)."
]

instance FrictionModelParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to friction model coefficients (e.g., Stribeck curve parameters) for improved accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LoadDependentFrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive control strategies based on real-time torque/velocity profiles for load-dependent friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceTriggerInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of anomalous friction patterns (e.g., sudden increases in τ_out deviations) indicating wear or misalignment."
]

instance TorqueDeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of deviations between measured system output torque (τ_out) and simulated frictionless torque (r̃_out)."
]

instance AngularVelocityDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of deviations between measured system output angular velocity (ω_out) and simulated frictionless angular velocity (r̃_ω_out)."
]

instance OptimizedFrictionParametersInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized friction model parameters dynamically adapted to account for wear, load, or temperature changes."
]

instance AdaptiveControlRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for manual integration into SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller to refine motion control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataSourceProvenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Full traceability of data sources (sensors/simulations) and processing provenance for each data point."
]

instance UnifiedDataVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified visualization of time-series data, model outputs, and deviation metrics in a single dashboard."
]

instance SystemComponentStructureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural decomposition of the first axis into permanent magnet synchronous motor, gear transmission, and cycloidal gearbox components."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is **unidirectional** (digital shadow acts as a **passive entity** per Kritzinger et al. [32]), meaning no direct feedback to the physical system is implemented. However, the digital shadow enables:
  - **Indirect influence via insights**: Friction model parameters (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous coefficients) can be **updated in the CNC controller (SINUMERIK 840D sl)** for improved motion control, though this requires **manual integration** of findings.
  - **Visualization dashboards**: Display **real-time comparisons** between measured and simulated data (e.g., *τ_out* vs. *τ̂_out*) to aid human operators in adjusting control strategies."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **twinning time-scale** is **real-time** with:
  - **Data acquisition rate**: Sensor data (e.g., *I_M*, encoder readings) streamed via **MQTT** at a frequency aligned with the **CNC controller’s control loop** (typically **1–10 kHz** for industrial robots).
  - **Processing latency**: Simulink-based friction model computations and data trace generation occur **synchronously** with operational data, enabling **online friction identification**.
  - **Synchronization**: The digital shadow operates on the **same time interval** as the physical system, with **System Properties** (e.g., configuration snapshots) stored for **retrospective analysis**."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **digital twin constellation** is orchestrated as follows:
  - **Centralized digital shadow** for the **first axis**, with **modular processing components** (*Simulation_M*, *Simulation_GW*, *Simulation_GB*) operating in parallel.
  - **Data flow**:
    1. **Physical system** (MABI MAX axis) → **Sensors** (encoders, current sensors) → **MQTT stream** → **Digital Shadow**.
    2. **Digital Shadow** processes data via **Simulink models** and **SysML-based structural models**.
    3. **Outputs** (e.g., *τ̂_out*, deviations) are visualized in a **dashboard** for human analysis.
  - **No closed-loop control**: The digital shadow is **passive** (per Kritzinger et al.), with no direct feedback to the physical system. **Indirect influence** occurs via **operator-driven adjustments** to CNC parameters based on insights."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **life-cycle stages** covered by the digital shadow include:
  - **As-operated phase**: The digital shadow is **purpose-built for real-time monitoring** of the **operational state** of the first axis, focusing on **friction dynamics** during machining tasks.
  - **Representation phases**:
    - **As-designed**: Structural models (SysML **Block Definition Diagrams**) describe the **ideal mechanical structure** (motor, gearboxes, connectors).
    - **As-manufactured**: Not explicitly modeled; assumes nominal parameters from manufacturer specs (e.g., gear ratios).
    - **As-operated**: Behavioral models (Simulink) and **live sensor data** capture **deviations due to wear, load, or temperature**, enabling **adaptive friction compensation**.
  - **Evolution**: The digital shadow supports **continuous model refinement** (e.g., updating friction parameters) but does not cover **design or manufacturing phases**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity and validity considerations** include:
  - **Model Fidelity**:
    - **White-box models** (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous) provide **physically interpretable friction descriptions** but require **parameter tuning**.
    - **Black-box models** (data-driven) avoid parameter identification but lack **explainability**.
    - **Hybrid approach**: Combines both for **robustness** (e.g., using Simulink to subtract friction effects from ideal models).
  - **Validation Mechanisms**:
    - **Deviation analysis**: Compare **measured data traces** (*r_out*) with **simulated traces** (*r̂_out*) to quantify friction-induced errors.
    - **Traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source** (sensor/simulation) and **processing chain**, enabling **root-cause analysis**.
    - **Uncertainty quantification**: Not explicitly addressed; relies on **manufacturer-provided dynamics models** and **sensor precision** (±0.1 mm positional accuracy).
  - **Error Sources**:
    - **Sensor noise** (encoder/current sensors).
    - **Model approximations** (e.g., simplified gearbox dynamics).
    - **Environmental factors** (temperature, load) not fully captured in models."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **digital twin technical connection** includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time, lightweight streaming** of sensor data (*I_M*, encoder angles) from the **SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller** to the digital shadow.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series arrays of **floating-point values** (torque *τ*, angular velocity *ω*, current *I_M*).
  - **Architecture**:
    - **Centralized processing**: Simulink models and SysML structures reside on a **single computational node** (e.g., PC/server).
    - **No direct PT-DT feedback**: Data flows **unidirectionally** from PT → DT (per digital shadow definition)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **digital twin hosting/deployment** involves:
  - **Execution Environment**: **MathWorks Simulink** (for real-time friction model computations) and **SysML tools** (e.g., Cameo Systems Modeler) for structural modeling.
  - **Dashboard**: Hosted on a **web-based platform** (technology unspecified) to display **time-series plots**, **deviation metrics**, and **model outputs**.
  - **Data Storage**: Not explicitly detailed; assumes **in-memory processing** for real-time analysis with **retrospective storage** of data traces for offline validation."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **standardization** efforts include:
  - **Modeling Standards**:
    - **SysML** (for structural/behavioral modeling, per [17]).
    - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (implicitly referenced for semantic data integration, though not detailed).
  - **Methodological Standards**:
    - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** (Fig. 1) as a **domain-specific reference model** for concretization in use cases.
    - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., **RFLP**, **OOSEM**) for system decomposition.
  - **Protocol Standards**:
    - **MQTT** for real-time data streaming (IETF standard).
  - **No explicit compliance**: The paper does not reference **industry-specific DT standards** (e.g., ISO 23247, NIST Framework)."
]
